issue_title: No man's sky crashes after trying to start a game
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: No man's sky
- Steam AppID of the game: 275850

## System Information
- GPU: AMD ATI Radeon RX 5600 OEM/5600 XT / 5700/5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa
- Kernel version: Linux 5.15.2-arch1-1 x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/arkindal/48d894cea66249249f07997622b6aa31)
- Proton version: 4.11-13 (but tried multiple versions)

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms 
I can't play, the game boots fine but when I try to actually start playing it crashes.


## Reproduction
Boot game
Click on play game OR multiplayer
Load a save file OR start a new one
The loading screen animation starts, it's very slow and extremely blurry, it shows it struggles to load the animation properly
Eventually an error screen pops out with this reference token: 79372_0x1E20A19_ST76561198032370916

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenc
[steam-275850.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7541399/steam-275850.log)
ed above.
-->
## Extra non required things
I don't know if this can be of any help, but here's the output I get from the terminal if I start steam from it and try to start the game.
https://pastebin.com/YdRZAhry
I've been trying to fix this problem for days now, I looked in many forums and chats, including and not limited to the proton discord server. I also tried multiple versions of proton, the problem changes ever so slightly but the end result is always the same.
The different outcomes I can get are that the loading screen animation doesn't even try to start and the game crashes right away or that the game doesn't even start at all.
symptom_summary:
用户在尝试启动《无人深空》时遇到崩溃问题，游戏能够正常启动，但在加载游戏时出现模糊的加载动画，最终导致错误屏幕弹出。 

Tags: Crash, Loading Issues, Visual Glitches

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Street Fighter 6
- Steam AppID of the game: 1364780

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX 6600
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.2.7
- Kernel version: 6.11.11-300.fc41
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/eOliverr/62cc84f1afe49687d904b599d818c7a0):
- Proton version: experimental-9.0-20241206

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1364780.log](https://gist.github.com/eOliverr/2b6475d75c3df87286ddb24bff36dca3)

## Symptoms
Game freezes itself as well as the whole os while in "Battle Hub" game mode.
The picture doesn't fully freeze, but the game is totally stuck and doesn't let me do anything.
Can't exit the game as a result, having to reboot the pc.
This happens to me every time I visit Battle Hub.

## Reproduction
Get into a Battle Hub lobby, interact with things for a moment, game freezes.
Freezes specifically happen when trying to interact with something like a cabinet or a shop, or while exiting a shop. 
It might not happen immediately, but usually it's very quick.
symptom_summary:
用户在“Battle Hub”游戏模式中遇到游戏和操作系统冻结的问题，游戏画面未完全冻结，但无法进行任何操作，导致必须重启电脑。每次进入Battle Hub时都会发生此问题。

Tags: Game Freeze, System Freeze, Unresponsive

issue_title: JOY OF PROGRAMMING - Software Engineering Simulator (2216770)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: JOY OF PROGRAMMING - Software Engineering Simulator
- Steam AppID of the game: 2216770

## System Information
- GPU: Radeon RX 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: mesa 24.0.2
- Kernel version: 6.7.6-273-tkg-eevdf
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/8ddabc7b0d0419af5f77d4e69c50fc96 and https://gist.github.com/Patola/5571720ffe575b067758d79e6f5be760
- Proton version: 8.0-5, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

- Game "crashes" / does not run properly

## Reproduction
- Start the game. A 3D representation of a laptop with the game's steam page appears, click on play and will be taken to another screen with many options. Click on the first choice, "START GAME", it immediately disappears to the desktop and the Steam game is still running, stays running indefinitely and it needs the "STOP" button to be pressed.

* Note: This seems to be a tough one to crack. This game comes with python 3.10.4 embedded for windows. Seems to use some chrome DLL too. It's likely it would have called an external executable when I clicked on "START GAME"
* Tried both DX11 and DX12 options when starting, same behavior.

[steam-2216770.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14462728/steam-2216770.log)

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Layers of Fear VR
- Steam AppID of the game: 1183030

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6800 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 20.3.4-1
- Kernel version: 5.10.15-120-tkg-bmq
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/6caf42ff20f69f1bc95b527ef7714a31
- Proton version: 5.13-6 (also tried 5.0-10)

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1183030.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/5977208/steam-1183030.log)
[steam_logs.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/5977225/steam_logs.zip)
![ohno](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2991620/107872237-225d4b80-6ea9-11eb-8959-7620beb76e9a.png)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Layers of Fear VR starts correctly, even starts playing. But after a few seconds of playing, it crashes steamvr completely, the game kind of continues playing but the HMD turns off and sound changes to the regular speakers. SteamVR says "Oh no! SteamVR has encountered an unexpected problem." (screenshot attached)

## Reproduction
- Start the game (Valve Index)
- You can choose "New Game", or "Continue". It will start loading.
- After loading you'll appear inside the house, just before the first door.  A few seconds later, no matter what you do or don't do, the game will crash.
- Since it seems it also screws SteamVR IPC, even restarting SteamVR will not make VR work. A reboot makes SteamVR work again.

Note: the current steam log has crashed even before the inside the house scene even appeared, shortly after the loading screen disappeared. I also included the most recent logs under ~/.steam (error.log and the files under steam/logs/) for good measure.
symptom_summary:
用户在游戏中遇到崩溃的问题，首先画面冻结但音频仍在工作，几秒后游戏关闭且没有错误信息。崩溃在城市区域更频繁发生。

Tags: Crash, Freeze, Audio Issues, Visual Glitches

issue_title: No Man's Sky (275850) Crashes etc. at random points
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: No Man's Sky
- Steam AppID of the game: 275850

## System Information
- GPU: AMD ATI Radeon RX 5600 OEM/5600 XT / 5700/5700 XT 
- Driver/LLVM version:   4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 21.1.4
- Kernel version: 5.12.12
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):  https://gist.github.com/Srogozins/61515238c382e3edc3f82f13533a212b 
[steam-275850.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7094920/steam-275850.log)
[dmesg.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7094921/dmesg.log)
https://gist.github.com/Srogozins/5d7485fec382b77a5551e5bc77c85b3b 
- Proton version: 5.0-10

## I confirm:
- [ x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

After some in-game, ranging from a few minutes to hours the game would often freeze, with visual artifacts appearing on the screen in and out of game/crash/crash the X-server/freeze the system to the point of needing to kill the X-server/cause the computer to reboot. Seems to occur during any sort of activity.

## Reproduction

Play the game for a few hours.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.

4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in

compatibility_report:
Elex II - DirectX 12 version

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6331.
@Xenophobe-01 posted on 2022-11-21T18:26:15:

# Compatibility Report
- Elex II - DirectX 12 mode
- Steam AppID 9000040

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: PPA 22.2.4-kisak-mesa
- Linux Mint 21, Kernel version: 5.15.0-53
- Link to full system information report as [Gist]:https://gist.github.com/Xenophobe-01/42d230975da00368ed7030102c8bd500
- Proton versions: 7.0-4 and Experimental

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms 
There is an odd fireball glitch that only occurs using the DX12 version of the game in Linux. This glitch does not occur with the DX12 version in windows. I'm submitting this report on the chance that this issue is caused by DX12 calls being converted to Vulkan.

## Reproduction
Start game in DX12 mode and play until you get to first meeting with Caya (does not take long to get there) and enter into dialog.  Here's a link to a Youtube clip of what I'm experiencing.  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yx2AA0L9lcY
[steam-900040.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10059527/steam-900040.log)
symptom_summary:
用户在Linux上使用Elex II的DirectX 12版本时遇到了奇怪的火球故障，该故障在Windows的DX12版本中不存在。该问题可能与DX12调用转换为Vulkan有关。

Tag suggestions: Visual Glitch, Graphics Issue, DirectX 12
